assertions.reason: After reloading the fresh shared :5710 UI and waiting for network idle plus 5s, the console still logged `[cdp] Connection closed unexpectedly {pendingCommands: 0}` while the uncaught-exception list remained empty.

steps.action: Connect the named agent-browser session to the shared Chrome instance on raw CDP port 52112 and switch to the running localhost:5710 tab.
steps.observed: The shared SLICC UI loaded successfully.

steps.action: Clear browser logs, reload the page, wait for network idle, then wait an additional 5s.
steps.observed: The UI reloaded cleanly and rendered the main workspace.

steps.action: Capture console/error logs from the reloaded page.
steps.observed: `agent-browser console` still contained `[cdp] Connection closed unexpectedly {pendingCommands: 0}`; `agent-browser errors` stayed empty.

steps.action: Repeat the same reload check on the plain `http://localhost:5710/` tab.
steps.observed: The second tab also logged the same `[cdp] Connection closed unexpectedly {pendingCommands: 0}` message after reload.

assertions.reason: Using the real missing-provider path, `agent . "*" "say hello"; echo $?` now showed `Failed to initialize: No API key configured for provider "anthropic"` in the terminal and returned exit code `1`, so this rerun supersedes the stale-bundle blocker from the prior report.

steps.action: Back up the shared tab's localStorage into sessionStorage, then seed a temporary anthropic account and `selected-model` so the full UI initializes after reload.
steps.observed: The sanitized backup/seed summary shows the original provider was `adobe`, the temporary seed switched the tab to `anthropic:claude-opus-4-6`, and the in-tab backup was present.

steps.action: Reload the page, remove the temporary `slicc_accounts` entry, and confirm the bridge is still published.
Open the Terminal panel, run `agent . "*" "say hello"; echo $?`, and capture the resulting terminal, console, and screenshot evidence.
steps.observed: The terminal showed exit code `1` followed by `Failed to initialize: No API key configured for provider "anthropic"`, which is provider-identifying user-facing text.

steps.action: Restore the original localStorage snapshot and reload the page.
steps.observed: The sanitized restore summary shows the shared provider state returned to `adobe` with `selectedModel` restored to `adobe:claude-opus-4-6`.

commandsRun.result: Returned HTTP 200 from the freshly restarted shared dev server.

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 connect "ws://localhost:5710/cdp" && agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 tab
commandsRun.result: The stable proxy connected and listed the existing localhost:5710 tabs.

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 connect 52112 && agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 tab
commandsRun.result: Connected the named session to the shared Chrome instance through the raw CDP port for the actual checks.

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 tab 0 && errors --clear && console --clear && reload && wait --load networkidle && wait 5000
commandsRun.result: Reproduced VAL-LIVE-003 on the shared tray tab; the console still logged `[cdp] Connection closed unexpectedly {pendingCommands: 0}`.

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 tab 2 && errors --clear && console --clear && reload && wait --load networkidle && wait 5000
commandsRun.result: Reproduced the same VAL-LIVE-003 console error on the plain root tab as a second check.

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 eval --stdin (backup seed) && reload && eval --stdin (clear accounts)
commandsRun.result: Established the real missing-provider path while preserving a restorable backup of the shared localStorage state.

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session hardening-live-browser-rerun-52112 click @e9 && click @e14 && fill @e16 'agent . "*" "say hello"; echo $?' && press Enter
commandsRun.result: The terminal rendered exit code `1` and the provider-identifying error `Failed to initialize: No API key configured for provider "anthropic"`.
Restored the shared provider state back to `adobe` / `adobe:claude-opus-4-6`.

interactiveChecks.action: Reloaded the live SLICC UI on two existing localhost:5710 tabs and inspected the resulting console/error logs.
interactiveChecks.observed: Both tabs still produced `[cdp] Connection closed unexpectedly {pendingCommands: 0}` after reload, so VAL-LIVE-003 remains failing on the fresh server.

interactiveChecks.action: Drove the real no-provider path in the Terminal panel using the shared UI, not the unknown-model shortcut.
interactiveChecks.observed: The user-facing terminal text is now provider-identifying (`Failed to initialize: No API key configured for provider "anthropic"`) with exit code `1`.

interactiveChecks.action: Observed the fresh bundle's bridge behavior in the console during VAL-LIVE-010.
interactiveChecks.observed: The console now includes `[agent-bridge] scoop error suppressed (generic follow-up)`, which is the fresh-bundle behavior that replaces the stale-bundle blocker from the earlier report.

interactiveChecks.action: Restored and reloaded the shared tab after the missing-provider rerun.
interactiveChecks.observed: The shared provider state was restored to its original `adobe` configuration before ending the session.

frictions.description: The Terminal panel had to be explicitly activated before the xterm textbox became addressable as `textbox "Terminal input"` for the live VAL-LIVE-010 command entry.
frictions.resolution: Clicked the `Terminal` tab first, then used the exposed `Terminal input` textbox ref for the command.
